## [00:00 - 03:04]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about what you're going to cover as you do your content section of your webinar. So as we get into this, I need to give you a warning. There are people out there who are selling courses on how to do webinar, who argue that you don't really need to give any content in a webinar. Now, here's the problem. Their webinars are content free, high hype, pitchy affairs that may sell a lot, but also have huge refunds afterwards. I will tell you every time I have had contact with one of those webinars, whether I'm as an attendee or I've had somebody who's pitched me on doing a webinar from my list, and I've listened to a sample webinar, it just makes me want to gag. Because I know that I am revolted by someone wasting my time just giving me a sales pitch. I have this belief that we have an obligation to deliver people what they expect, which is content that will help them to improve their lives. Whether it's their lives or their businesses, whatever that is, we have a responsibility to give them what they're hoping for. And I can almost guarantee they're not hoping for a sales pitch. And so when you look at this, in fact, I've actually seen courses on how to do great webinars, that one of the modules is how to minimize returns. What they're actually saying is, when you bring somebody in and you manipulate them in divine, and they now want to return, here's how you keep them manipulated so that they don't return and they don't complain. And it's like, there's just something wrong with that. And so I assure you that's not what this program is about. Because you're going to make a lot more in total if you'll make the focus of the content portion of your webinar, both to build trust and to prepare them to buy. Both of these are vital. If all you do is build trust, they're not ready to buy and they're not going to buy very well. But if all you do is prepare them to buy, which is what those webinar people are saying is, hey, we got it. The purpose of the first half of the webinar is just to get them ready to buy. And you don't really need to give them any good information because they're going to get that in the course.

## [06:11 - 09:18]

And I ask you as the listener on this webinar to make commitments back to me. Next we go into the reframe or opportunity portion of the webinar and you're going to spend a lot of time thinking about this. Basically what we're doing here is we are changing the way that they think about their situation in life. And that change in the way that they're thinking can be done in a number of ways. But this sets up everything else that you're going to tell them after this point. So if you come in telling someone hey you don't believe you can do this you're wrong. Guess what as soon as you tell them they're wrong. Their hackles are up and they're not interested in listening to you at the same level as if you're able to come in and reframe their thought process in a different way. And so they're saying oh maybe I can. And that's what we do in this reframe. The other way of doing this is saying hey there's a huge opportunity that is not being met out there. And here's what that opportunity is. Here's what it means. And I'm going to show you in a moment how you can take advantage of that opportunity. Next after the reframe or opportunity we go into your story. So here is where you briefly talk about why you are the one presenting this webinar to them. Now one of the challenges is that a lot of people spend 20 minutes doing that part. Which is one of the reasons why we do the reframe before that because the reframe actually becomes content. It's what causes them to look at their world differently. And so now we can tell our story in a way that people don't say it's been 20 minutes and this person hasn't said a word that's worth writing down. And so this story we're doing a hero's journey story and I will describe all that in a lot of detail on how exactly we do that.
